channel.title: Artometrics Podcast
channel.description: Interviews extending the Artometrics desks — culture, atlas, history, persona, power.
channel.itunes:author: Artometrics
channel.itunes:summary: Data-science conversations for the creative industries.

item.title: Why culture needs a data desk
item.description: Juliet Ramos on treating anime catalogs, streaming libraries, and franchise economics as public evidence — not just fandom.

item.title: Reading an Artometrics report from scratch
item.description: David Lee walks through observed data, derived metrics, editorial indices, and outside literature — the four evidence types behind every report.

item.title: Long timelines, small samples: history desk lessons
item.description: Isaac Turner on working with archival gaps, imperial datasets, and the humility required when the past does not arrive in tidy rows.

item.title: Franchise economics and the persona of a dynasty
item.description: Jordan Wells on measuring sports and creator dynasties — wins, revenue, platform reach, and why myth needs a spreadsheet.
